# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: terrain, terrain assessment, pre-intervention, checklist, why protocol fails, protocol not working, intervention readiness, treatment not working, NAD not working, IV not working, peptides not working, hormones not working, fat loss not working, nothing works, tried everything, hormonal OS, thyroid conversion, FT3, rT3, low T3, reverse T3, cortisol, cortisol low, cortisol AM limitation, cortisol single snapshot, DUTCH test cortisol, salivary cortisol, adrenal, HPA axis, testosterone, low T, insulin resistance, HOMA-IR, metabolic, inflammation, hsCRP, CRP high, IL-6, homocysteine, methylation, cofactors, magnesium, Mg RBC, ferritin, iron, anemia, hemoglobin, sleep apnea, oxygen, gut permeability, leaky gut, zonulin, Track B, before IV, before peptides, before hormones, before protocol, terrain first, Resimont, biological terrain, prepare terrain, fix terrain, correct terrain, red terrain, yellow terrain, green terrain, CD38, PARP, mitochondria blocked, exogenous, iatrogenic, drug effect, medication side effect, substance use, excess hormone, supraphysiological, steroid effect, polypharmacy effect, statin depletion, medication-induced
# CATEGORY: Clinical Tools
# USE FOR: Pre-intervention assessment, why treatments fail, terrain readiness, Track B preparation, protocol optimization
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 07_MITOCHONDRIA, 08_LONGEVITY_NAD, 09_HPA_ADRENAL, 57_THYROID_COMPLETE, 06_METABOLIC, 12_GUT, 36_TESTING_GUIDE, 50_DIAGNOSTIC_DECISION_TREES, 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS, 66_LAB_ANALYSIS_ENGINE, 67_GLUTEN_CELIAC_NCGS
# RED_FLAGS: Hb <8 (severe anemia - urgent), Cortisol AM <5 (adrenal crisis risk), TSH >10 or <0.1 (severe thyroid dysfunction)
# TRIAGE_QUESTIONS: What have you tried already? How long without improvement? Any recent labs?
# IV_ROLE: This file determines WHETHER and WHEN IV is appropriate. Terrain assessment comes BEFORE IV recommendation. If terrain is RED, delay IV and correct terrain first.
# INTERVENTION_PRIORITY: Assess terrain → Identify blockers → Correct critical issues → Start protocol on prepared terrain
# TELECONSULT_LIMITS: CAN do remotely: full terrain assessment, lab ordering, result interpretation, correction protocols, follow-up. CANNOT do remotely: physical exam. Note: terrain assessment is 100% teleconsult-compatible.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TERRAIN ASSESSMENT — PRE-INTERVENTION READINESS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## WHY PROTOCOLS SOMETIMES DON'T WORK

You've probably heard stories: someone does an expensive IV protocol, takes 
all the right supplements, follows the program perfectly — and nothing happens.
Or worse, they feel the same or even worse than before.

**The problem isn't usually the protocol. It's the terrain.**

Think of it like trying to grow plants in bad soil. You can have the best 
seeds, the best fertilizer, perfect watering schedule — but if the soil is 
toxic, depleted, or waterlogged, nothing grows well.

**Your body works the same way.**

Before we start any serious program, we need to make sure your biological 
terrain can actually RESPOND to the intervention. Otherwise, we're wasting 
your time and money.

---

## WHAT IS "TERRAIN"?

Your terrain is the internal environment of your body — the conditions that 
determine whether treatments work or fail.

**The 4 Layers of Terrain:**

**1. HORMONAL OPERATING SYSTEM**
Your hormones (thyroid, cortisol, testosterone, insulin) are like the 
operating system of a computer. If the OS is crashed, it doesn't matter 
how good the software is — nothing runs properly.

**2. INFLAMMATION LEVEL**
Chronic inflammation blocks healing, blocks signaling, blocks response 
to almost any intervention. It's like trying to repair a house while 
it's still on fire.

**3. COFACTORS & BUILDING BLOCKS**
Your body needs raw materials — magnesium, iron, B vitamins, zinc — to 
run its biochemistry. If these are depleted, enzymes can't work, and 
nothing functions optimally.

**4. OXYGEN & NUTRIENT DELIVERY**
If you're anemic or have sleep apnea, your tissues are starved of oxygen 
every day. No amount of supplements fixes that.

---

## WHEN DO WE CHECK TERRAIN?

**Track A (Acute):** Bali Belly, hangover, jet lag, simple dehydration
→ Skip terrain. Fix the immediate problem.

**Track B (Chronic/Optimization):** Fatigue programs, anti-aging, hormone 
optimization, fat loss, peptide therapy, detox, athletic performance
→ ALWAYS check terrain first.

If you've "tried everything and nothing works," the terrain is almost 
certainly the issue.

---

## WHAT WE CHECK

| Layer | What We Test | Why It Matters |
|-------|--------------|----------------|
| Hormonal | FT3, rT3, Cortisol AM, Testosterone, Insulin | Controls all downstream processes |
| Inflammatory | hsCRP, Homocysteine | Blocks healing and response |
| Cofactors | Mg RBC, Ferritin, B12 | Raw materials for biochemistry |
| Delivery | Hemoglobin, Sleep screening | Oxygen to tissues |

---

## WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TERRAIN ASSESSMENT

**GREEN TERRAIN:** All systems functional
→ Proceed with your protocol. It will work.

**YELLOW TERRAIN:** 1-2 minor issues
→ We can start the protocol AND fix the terrain issues in parallel.

**RED TERRAIN:** 2+ issues OR 1 critical blocker
→ We fix the terrain FIRST. Then start the protocol.
→ This usually takes 2-4 weeks.
→ It's not a delay — it's making sure the protocol actually works.

---

## COMMON TERRAIN BLOCKERS

**"I've tried NAD+ / peptides / hormones and felt nothing"**
→ Check: FT3/rT3, Cortisol AM, hsCRP, Mg RBC

**"I can't lose weight no matter what I do"**
→ Check: HOMA-IR, FT3/rT3, Cortisol pattern, Sleep apnea

**"I'm exhausted all the time, supplements don't help"**
→ Check: Ferritin (not just Hb), FT3/rT3, Cortisol AM, Homocysteine

**"I recovered from illness but never got my energy back"**
→ Check: rT3 (often elevated post-illness), Cortisol, Inflammation markers

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## THE RESIMONT PRINCIPLE

Dr. Vincent Resimont's core insight: **Hormones are the operating system. 
The body is the hardware. Without correct hormonal signals, no hardware 
upgrade works.**

This applies to ALL Track B interventions:
- NAD+ / NMN / IV therapy
- Peptides (BPC-157, Cerebrolysin, GH secretagogues)
- Hormone optimization (TRT, thyroid, DHEA)
- Fat loss programs
- Detox protocols
- Athletic performance optimization
- Gut healing protocols
- Anti-aging programs

**A failing protocol is almost always a TERRAIN problem, not a dosing problem.**

---

## TERRAIN LAYER 1: HORMONAL OPERATING SYSTEM

The master regulators. If broken, everything downstream fails.

### THYROID (FT3, FT4, rT3, TSH)

| Finding | Interpretation | Action |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| TSH normal, FT3 low-normal, rT3 elevated | Conversion blocked (common post-illness, stress) | Address cause, support conversion (selenium, zinc, stress reduction). Consider T3 if refractory. |
| TSH normal, FT3 low-normal, rT3 normal | Subclinical hypothyroid | Optimize selenium, zinc, iodine (carefully), iron. Recheck 6-8 weeks. |
| TSH elevated >4, FT4 low | Overt hypothyroidism | Medical management (levothyroxine). We optimize terrain around it. |
| FT3 in upper third, rT3 normal | GREEN — thyroid terrain optimal | Proceed with protocol. |

**Why rT3 matters:** Reverse T3 blocks T3 at the receptor. Patient can have 
"normal thyroid labs" but cells are hypothyroid. This is THE most common 
missed cause of treatment-resistant fatigue.

**Common causes of high rT3:** Acute illness, chronic stress, calorie 
restriction, inflammation, medications (beta-blockers, amiodarone).

### ADRENAL (Cortisol AM, DHEA-S)

| Finding | Interpretation | Action |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| Cortisol AM <5 µg/dL | CRITICAL — adrenal crisis risk | Urgent endocrinology. Do NOT start any protocol. |
| Cortisol AM 5-10 µg/dL | Suggestive of insufficiency | Confirm with DUTCH or 4-point salivary BEFORE classifying as RED. Correlate with DHEA-S and symptoms. |
| Cortisol AM 10-15 µg/dL | Suboptimal, HPA may be stressed | Support with adaptogens, sleep, stress management. Can proceed cautiously. |
| Cortisol AM >15 µg/dL | GREEN — adrenal terrain adequate | Proceed with protocol. |
| DHEA-S low for age | Adrenal reserve depleted | Consider DHEA 25-50mg/day after baseline documented. |

**SINGLE AM CORTISOL LIMITATION:** A single fasting AM cortisol is a
SCREENING tool, not a diagnostic tool. Values between 5-10 µg/dL require
confirmation because cortisol is affected by: wake time, sleep quality the
prior night, acute stress, recent illness, and medications. A value of
8-10 µg/dL in an otherwise asymptomatic patient without low DHEA-S may
reflect timing artifact rather than true adrenal insufficiency. Confirm
with 4-point salivary cortisol or DUTCH test before gating protocols.

**When to treat as RED without confirmation:** Cortisol <10 AND DHEA-S low
AND clinical symptoms (profound fatigue, salt cravings, orthostatic
intolerance, inability to handle stress). This triad is sufficient to
classify RED pending confirmation.

**When to treat as YELLOW pending confirmation:** Cortisol 8-10 AND DHEA-S
not available AND patient is otherwise functional. Order DUTCH/salivary
to determine true status. Low-risk interventions (B-complex IV, oral
supplements, lifestyle) may proceed. High-intensity protocols (NAD+ IV,
intensive peptide programs) should wait for confirmation.

**Why cortisol matters:** Crashed cortisol = profound fatigue unresponsive 
to ANY intervention. NAD+, peptides, hormones — nothing works until cortisol 
is addressed. Post-ICU, post-burnout, post-chronic-stress patients commonly 
have this.

### GONADAL (Testosterone, SHBG, Estradiol)

| Finding | Interpretation | Action |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| Total T low, Free T low, SHBG normal | True hypogonadism | Evaluate for TRT after checklist passed |
| Total T normal, Free T low, SHBG high | SHBG trapping testosterone | Address root cause (insulin, liver, thyroid). May need TRT despite "normal" total. |
| T low + high E2 | Aromatization issue | Address with lifestyle, DIM, or AI if needed. |
| Free T in upper third | GREEN — gonadal terrain optimal | Proceed with protocol. |

**Why testosterone matters:** T regulates anabolism, repair, mood, energy, 
muscle, fat distribution. Low T = poor response to training, peptides, 
optimization protocols.

### METABOLIC (Fasting Insulin, HOMA-IR, HbA1c)

| Finding | Interpretation | Action |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| HbA1c <5.7%, Fasting insulin <8, HOMA-IR <2 | GREEN — metabolic terrain optimal | Proceed. |
| HbA1c controlled but insulin >15, HOMA-IR >2.5 | Insulin resistance (hidden diabetic) | Prioritize IR: berberine, ALA, exercise, carb control. |
| HbA1c >6.5%, on medications | Overt diabetes | Medical management. We optimize terrain around it. CGM required for ALA. |

**Why HOMA-IR matters:** HbA1c can be 6.8% with insulin at 45 = MASSIVE 
resistance. The cells are starving despite glucose availability. Inflammation 
up, signaling blocked, fat loss impossible.

---

## TERRAIN LAYER 2: INFLAMMATORY LOAD

Chronic inflammation blocks healing, blocks signaling, blocks everything.

### MARKERS

| Marker | Green | Yellow | Red | Action if Elevated |
|--------|-------|--------|-----|-------------------|
| hsCRP | <1 mg/L | 1-3 mg/L | >5 mg/L | Find source (gut, infection, autoimmune, metabolic). Anti-inflammatory protocol. |
| IL-6 | Normal | Mildly elevated | Significantly elevated | Source investigation. Quercetin, curcumin, omega-3, lifestyle. |
| Homocysteine | <10 µmol/L | 10-12 µmol/L | >15 µmol/L | B12, folate (methylfolate if MTHFR), B6, TMG. Recheck 8 weeks. |

**Why hsCRP >5 blocks interventions:**
- Upregulates CD38 → consumes NAD+ faster than you can supply it
- Blocks cellular signaling
- Impairs healing and repair
- Creates catabolic environment
- Blocks fat loss

**Why homocysteine matters:**
- Marker of methylation capacity
- Methylation needed for: hormone metabolism, detox, neurotransmitters, DNA repair
- If methylation broken → many pathways fail
- High-dose NMN + broken methylation = metabolite accumulation

### COMMON INFLAMMATION SOURCES

1. **Gut permeability** — LPS leak → systemic inflammation
2. **Chronic infections** — Dental, sinus, gut, viral reactivation
3. **Insulin resistance** — Metabolic inflammation
4. **Visceral fat** — Adipokine-driven inflammation
5. **Poor sleep** — Inflammatory cytokine surge
6. **Chronic stress** — HPA-immune axis dysfunction
7. **Autoimmunity** — Underlying autoimmune process

---

## TERRAIN LAYER 3: COFACTORS & OXYGEN

The building blocks and delivery systems.

### COFACTORS

| Cofactor | Test | Green | Red | Why It Matters |
|----------|------|-------|-----|----------------|
| Magnesium | Mg RBC (NOT serum) | Upper half | Low | 300+ enzymatic reactions. ATP synthesis. NMNAT function. |
| Iron | Ferritin | >50 ng/mL | <30 ng/mL | O2 transport. Thyroid synthesis. Energy. |
| B12 | Serum B12 or MMA | >500 pg/mL | <300 pg/mL | Methylation. Neuro. Energy. |
| Folate | RBC folate | Adequate | Low | Methylation partner to B12. |
| Zinc | Plasma zinc | Normal | Low | Thyroid conversion. Immune. Enzymatic. |
| Copper/Zinc | Cu/Zn ratio | Balanced | Imbalanced | Immune. Thyroid. Antioxidant enzymes. |

**Why Mg RBC, not serum:** Serum Mg is tightly regulated and unreliable. 
You can have normal serum Mg with severe intracellular depletion.

**Why Ferritin >50, not just "normal":** Lab "normal" is 12-150. But ferritin 
<50 = functional iron deficiency. Thyroid hormone synthesis impaired. O2 
delivery suboptimal. Common cause of treatment-resistant fatigue in women.

### OXYGEN DELIVERY

| Issue | Screen | Green | Red | Action |
|-------|--------|-------|-----|--------|
| Anemia | Hemoglobin | Hb ≥12 (F), ≥13 (M) | Hb <10 | Correct anemia BEFORE intensive protocols |
| Sleep Apnea | Nocturnal oximetry, questionnaire | No desaturation | Desaturations present | CPAP before expecting full protocol benefit |

**Why anemia blocks everything:** O2 is the terminal electron acceptor in 
the mitochondrial ETC. No O2 = no ATP. No amount of NAD+ or CoQ10 fixes this.

**Why sleep apnea matters:** Nocturnal hypoxia every night = recovery blocked 
every night. Common in: elderly, overweight, heart failure, post-menopausal women.

---

## TERRAIN LAYER 4: GUT INTEGRITY

The gut is often the SOURCE of systemic inflammation.

### MARKERS

| Marker | What It Shows | When to Test | Action if Positive |
|--------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|
| Zonulin | Gut permeability | Chronic fatigue, autoimmune, persistent inflammation | 4R Protocol (Remove, Replace, Reinoculate, Repair) |
| Calprotectin | Gut inflammation | GI symptoms, IBD screening | GI workup, anti-inflammatory |
| GI-MAP | Comprehensive stool | Chronic gut issues, post-antibiotic | Targeted treatment based on findings |
| SIBO Breath Test | Small intestinal overgrowth | Bloating, fatigue, malabsorption | Antimicrobial protocol, motility support |

**Why gut matters for terrain:**
- Leaky gut → LPS enters bloodstream → systemic inflammation → CD38 up → NAD+ down
- Dysbiosis → malabsorption → cofactor deficiencies
- Gut-brain axis → mood, cognition, fatigue
- 20% of T4→T3 conversion happens in healthy gut

**When to prioritize gut in terrain assessment:**
- Chronic antibiotic history
- Post-sepsis, post-ICU
- Persistent inflammation despite interventions
- Autoimmune conditions
- Chronic fatigue with GI symptoms

---

## TERRAIN CLASSIFICATION

After assessing all 4 layers:

### GREEN TERRAIN
- All hormonal axes functional (FT3 good, cortisol >15, T adequate, HOMA-IR <2.5)
- Inflammation low (hsCRP <3, homocysteine <12)
- Cofactors adequate (Mg, ferritin, B12 sufficient)
- No anemia, no untreated apnea

**→ Proceed with protocol. It will work.**

### YELLOW TERRAIN
- 1-2 minor issues identified
- No critical blockers

**→ Start protocol + correct terrain issues IN PARALLEL**
Examples:
- Low Mg but everything else good → Start Mg, proceed with protocol
- Borderline ferritin → Start iron, proceed with protocol
- Mild inflammation → Add anti-inflammatory support, proceed with protocol

### RED TERRAIN
- 2+ significant issues
- OR 1 critical blocker:
  - Hb <10 (significant anemia)
  - FT3 crashed or rT3 very high
  - Cortisol AM <10
  - hsCRP >10
  - Homocysteine >20

**→ Do NOT start intensive protocol. It will fail.**
**→ Fix terrain FIRST. Usually 2-4 weeks.**
**→ Then launch protocol on prepared terrain.**

---

## PRE-INTERVENTION CHECKLISTS

### CORE TERRAIN CHECKLIST (ALL Track B)

| # | Checkpoint | Green | Red → Action |
|---|-----------|-------|--------------|
| 1 | Hemoglobin | ≥10 g/dL | <10: correct anemia |
| 2 | FT3 | Upper third | Low/rT3 high: address |
| 3 | Cortisol AM | >15 µg/dL | <10: adrenal workup |
| 4 | hsCRP | <3 mg/L | >5: anti-inflammatory |
| 5 | Mg RBC | Adequate | Low: correct |
| 6 | Homocysteine | <12 µmol/L | >15: B12/folate |
| 7 | Ferritin | >50 ng/mL | <30: iron repletion |

**Decision rule:** 2+ RED = fix terrain before starting intensive protocol.

### PRE-IV THERAPY
☐ Core terrain checklist
☐ Kidney function adequate (eGFR >30, >60 for high-dose Vit C)
☐ No active infection
☐ If diabetic on ALA: CGM in place

### PRE-PEPTIDE THERAPY
☐ Core terrain checklist
☐ Inflammation controlled (hsCRP <5)
☐ No active cancer (theoretical GH/growth factor concern)
☐ Clear therapeutic goal defined

### PRE-FAT LOSS PROGRAM
☐ FT3/rT3 checked (thyroid conversion)
☐ HOMA-IR assessed (insulin resistance)
☐ Cortisol pattern evaluated
☐ Sleep apnea screened
☐ Realistic timeline set

### PRE-DETOX PROTOCOL
☐ Homocysteine <12 (methylation functional)
☐ Liver function adequate
☐ Kidney function adequate
☐ Gut integrity assessed
☐ Not acutely stressed

### PRE-TRT
☐ PSA baseline
☐ Hematocrit <50%
☐ Fertility discussed
☐ Sleep apnea screened
☐ No active prostate cancer
☐ CV risk assessed

### PRE-T3 SUPPLEMENTATION
☐ rT3 elevated confirmed
☐ Selenium + iodine tried first
☐ Underlying cause identified
☐ No unstable angina
☐ Start low (5mcg)

---

## DECISION TREES BY FINDING

### rT3 ELEVATED (>15 ng/dL or ratio FT3:rT3 <0.2)

```
rT3 elevated
    │
    ├── Acute illness present?
    │   └── YES → Wait for recovery. rT3 will normalize. Don't treat.
    │
    ├── Chronic stress / HPA dysfunction?
    │   └── YES → Address stress, sleep, adaptogens first.
    │
    ├── Caloric restriction / dieting?
    │   └── YES → Increase calories. Reverse dieting.
    │
    ├── Medications (beta-blockers, amiodarone)?
    │   └── YES → Consider alternatives with prescriber.
    │
    ├── Inflammation elevated?
    │   └── YES → Address inflammation source first.
    │
    └── All above addressed, rT3 still high after 6-8 weeks?
        └── Consider T3 supplementation (5mcg start, monitor HR).
            Refer to endocrinology if uncertain.
```

### hsCRP >5 mg/L

```
hsCRP elevated
    │
    ├── Active infection?
    │   └── YES → Treat infection. Recheck CRP after resolution.
    │
    ├── GI symptoms present?
    │   └── YES → Gut workup (calprotectin, zonulin, GI-MAP).
    │             Address gut before proceeding.
    │
    ├── Insulin resistance (HOMA-IR >2.5)?
    │   └── YES → Metabolic inflammation. Address IR.
    │
    ├── Visceral obesity?
    │   └── YES → Fat loss priority (but check other terrain first).
    │
    ├── Autoimmune suspected?
    │   └── YES → ANA, specific antibodies. Rheumatology if positive.
    │
    ├── Dental / sinus issues?
    │   └── YES → Address chronic infections.
    │
    └── Source unclear?
        └── Trial anti-inflammatory protocol:
            - Omega-3 3-4g/day
            - Curcumin 1g/day
            - Quercetin 500mg/day
            - Address sleep, stress
            Recheck 6-8 weeks.
```

### CORTISOL AM <10 µg/dL

```
Cortisol AM low
    │
    ├── On corticosteroids or recent taper?
    │   └── YES → Iatrogenic suppression. Slow taper. Endocrine consult.
    │
    ├── Recent ICU / critical illness?
    │   └── YES → Post-ICU adrenal suppression. Time + support.
    │             Do NOT start intensive protocols.
    │
    ├── Chronic severe stress / burnout history?
    │   └── YES → HPA axis exhaustion. Rest, adaptogens, gradual recovery.
    │
    ├── Symptoms of adrenal crisis risk?
    │   (Severe weakness, hypotension, confusion)
    │   └── YES → URGENT. ER evaluation.
    │
    └── Stable patient, unexplained low cortisol?
        └── Synacthen stimulation test.
            Endocrinology referral.
            Consider low-dose hydrocortisone only after confirmation.
```

### HOMA-IR >2.5 (Insulin Resistance)

```
HOMA-IR elevated
    │
    ├── Lifestyle addressable?
    │   └── YES → First-line:
    │             - Carb reduction (not elimination)
    │             - Exercise (resistance + Zone 2)
    │             - Sleep optimization
    │             - Stress management
    │             Recheck 8-12 weeks.
    │
    ├── Lifestyle optimized, still elevated?
    │   └── Add:
    │       - Berberine 500mg 2-3x/day (check drug interactions)
    │       - ALA 600mg/day (CGM if diabetic)
    │       - Mg glycinate 400mg
    │       - Chromium 200-400mcg
    │       Recheck 8 weeks.
    │
    └── Refractory IR?
        └── Consider GLP-1 agonist discussion.
            Endocrinology / metabolic specialist.
```

### FERRITIN <30 ng/mL

```
Ferritin low
    │
    ├── Menstruating woman?
    │   └── Common cause. Iron bisglycinate 25-50mg + Vit C.
    │       Consider heavy period workup if severe.
    │
    ├── GI symptoms / malabsorption?
    │   └── Check celiac, H. pylori, gut integrity.
    │       Treat cause + supplement.
    │
    ├── Vegetarian / vegan?
    │   └── Dietary iron inadequate. Supplement.
    │
    ├── No obvious cause?
    │   └── Rule out occult GI blood loss (stool occult blood).
    │       Supplement while investigating.
    │
    └── Supplement protocol:
        Iron bisglycinate 25-50mg
        Take with Vitamin C 500mg
        Away from coffee/tea/calcium
        Recheck ferritin 8-12 weeks
        Target >50-70 ng/mL
```

---

## TERRAIN CORRECTION PROTOCOLS

### ANTI-INFLAMMATORY PROTOCOL (hsCRP >5)

**Oral:**
- Omega-3 (EPA+DHA) 3-4g/day with meals
- Curcumin (with piperine or liposomal) 1g/day
- Quercetin 500mg 2x/day
- Vitamin D optimize to 50-70 ng/mL

**Lifestyle:**
- Sleep 7-8h minimum (inflammation surges with poor sleep)
- Stress management (cortisol-inflammation axis)
- Remove inflammatory foods (processed, fried, high sugar)
- Anti-inflammatory diet (Mediterranean pattern)

**Address source:** Gut, dental, metabolic, autoimmune — find and treat.

**Timeline:** Recheck hsCRP at 6-8 weeks.

### METHYLATION SUPPORT (Homocysteine >12)

**Oral:**
- Methylcobalamin (B12) 1000-5000mcg/day
- Methylfolate 400-800mcg/day (NOT folic acid if MTHFR suspected)
- B6 (P5P) 50mg/day
- TMG (Betaine) 500-1000mg/day if >15
- Riboflavin 25-50mg/day

**Note:** Start low if patient is sensitive. Some feel worse initially 
as methylation "turns on."

**Timeline:** Recheck homocysteine at 8 weeks. Target <10.

### THYROID CONVERSION SUPPORT (rT3 elevated, FT3 low-normal)

**Address root causes first:** Stress, inflammation, calorie restriction.

**Oral:**
- Selenium 200mcg/day (Brazil nuts or supplement)
- Zinc 15-30mg/day
- Iodine — ONLY if confirmed deficient (can worsen Hashimoto)
- Iron optimize (ferritin >70 for optimal conversion)
- Ashwagandha 300-600mg (supports thyroid and stress)

**Lifestyle:**
- Adequate calories (restriction blocks conversion)
- Stress management (cortisol blocks conversion)
- Sleep optimization

**Timeline:** Recheck full thyroid panel (FT3, rT3, TSH) at 8 weeks.

### COFACTOR REPLETION

**Magnesium:**
- Mg glycinate 400mg at bedtime (or split 200mg 2x/day)
- Duration: ongoing for most people
- Recheck Mg RBC at 8-12 weeks

**Iron (if ferritin <50):**
- Iron bisglycinate 25-50mg
- With Vitamin C 500mg for absorption
- Away from coffee/tea/calcium/thyroid meds (2h separation)
- Recheck ferritin at 8-12 weeks
- Target 50-70+ ng/mL

**B12 (if <500 pg/mL):**
- Methylcobalamin 1000-5000mcg sublingual or oral
- Or B12 injections if malabsorption
- Recheck at 8 weeks

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## TERRAIN PANEL (Order for Track B patients)

**TIER 0 — TERRAIN TESTS:**
- FT3, FT4, rT3, TSH (full thyroid conversion)
- Cortisol AM (8am)
- Testosterone (total + free), SHBG
- Fasting insulin + glucose (calculate HOMA-IR)
- hsCRP
- Homocysteine
- Mg RBC
- Ferritin
- CBC (Hb, Hct)
- Vitamin D 25-OH

**ADD IF INDICATED:**
- IL-6 (if hsCRP elevated, source unclear)
- DHEA-S (if cortisol borderline or low)
- Estradiol sensitive (if T being assessed)
- Zonulin, Calprotectin (if gut suspected)
- Sleep study / nocturnal oximetry (if apnea suspected)

---

## QUICK CLASSIFICATION TABLE

| Marker | GREEN | YELLOW | RED |
|--------|-------|--------|-----|
| Hemoglobin | ≥12 (F), ≥13 (M) | 10-12 (F), 10-13 (M) | <10 |
| FT3 | Upper third of range | Mid-range | Lower third or rT3 high |
| Cortisol AM | >15 µg/dL | 10-15 µg/dL | <10 µg/dL |
| hsCRP | <1 mg/L | 1-3 mg/L | >5 mg/L |
| Homocysteine | <10 µmol/L | 10-12 µmol/L | >15 µmol/L |
| HOMA-IR | <2 | 2-2.5 | >2.5 |
| Mg RBC | Upper half | Lower half | Below range |
| Ferritin | >70 ng/mL | 30-70 ng/mL | <30 ng/mL |

**Scoring:** Count REDs
- 0-1 RED = GREEN TERRAIN → Proceed
- 2+ RED = RED TERRAIN → Fix first

---

## ONE-LINER DECISION RULES

- **rT3 high + stressed** → Address stress first, not thyroid meds
- **hsCRP >5** → Find inflammation source before protocols
- **Cortisol AM <10** → Do NOT start intensive protocols, workup first
- **Ferritin <30** → Supplement iron before expecting energy protocols to work
- **HOMA-IR >2.5** → Fat loss will stall until IR addressed
- **Homocysteine >15** → Fix methylation before high-dose anything
- **Hb <10** → Correct anemia before all else

---

## TERRAIN ASSESSMENT TALKING POINTS

**Opening:**
"Before we start the protocol, I want to make sure your system is ready to 
actually USE it. Sometimes the issue isn't the treatment — it's that the 
body can't respond because something upstream is blocked."

**If terrain is RED:**
"Your labs show [X and Y] that would block the protocol from working properly. 
I'd rather spend 2-4 weeks correcting that first — then when we start, you'll 
actually see results instead of wondering why it's not working."

**If terrain is YELLOW:**
"You have a couple minor things we should optimize, but nothing that blocks 
us from starting. We'll correct those in parallel with the protocol."

**If terrain is GREEN:**
"Your terrain looks good — hormones, inflammation, cofactors all in range. 
We can proceed with the protocol and expect it to work."

---

CEPAT SEHAT — Integrative & Regenerative Medicine
Reviv Bali | Infusion Phuket
